Global (All).title: Iran Unrest Escalates with Death Toll Rising to 58
Global (All).summary: Protests continue in Iran following the death of 22-year-old Mahsa Amini in police custody, with at least 58 deaths confirmed and multiple cities in lockdown.

Global (All).title: Russia-Ukraine Conflict Intensifies with Heavy Fighting
Global (All).summary: Heavy fighting was reported in several areas along the Russia-Ukraine border, with both sides exchanging artillery fire and reporting casualties.

Global (All).title: Global Supply Chain Disruptions Worsen Due to Shanghai Lockdown
Global (All).summary: The ongoing COVID-19 lockdown in Shanghai has severely impacted global supply chains, with multiple container ships being held offshore and thousands of workers required to isolate or quarantine.

United States.title: House Jan. 6 committee's final report on the 2021 U.S. Capitol attack is released
United States.summary: The report concluded that Trump's false claims of election fraud contributed to the violence on January 6, 2021.

United States.title: FBI Director Wray testifies before Senate Judiciary Committee amid controversy over Trump-era surveillance
United States.summary: Wray defended the FBI's actions and expressed concerns about the impact of the controversy on national security.

United States.title: Supreme Court Justice Kavanaugh tests positive for COVID-19, casting doubt on upcoming cases
United States.summary: The positive test is unlikely to have a significant impact on the court's schedule, but could lead to changes in case assignments.

South Korea.title: South Korea, North Korea exchange gunfire at border, with no reported casualties
South Korea.summary: The countries have been exchanging gunfire at their heavily militarized border since July 18.

South Korea.title: South Korean President Yoon orders probe into alleged embezzlement by his sister
South Korea.summary: The probe relates to alleged embezzlement of public funds in the city of Suncheon, where Yoon's sister serves as mayor.

South Korea.title: North Korean hackers linked to hacking of South Korean cryptocurrency exchanges
South Korea.summary: The hackers are believed to have stolen millions of dollars in cryptocurrency from exchanges in South Korea in recent months.

Japan.title: Typhoon Hinnamor Hits Japan's Southern Islands
Japan.summary: A powerful typhoon made landfall in the southern Japanese islands of Okinawa on Tuesday, causing widespread damage and disruption.

Japan.title: Japan-Australia Defence Relationship Deepens
Japan.summary: Japan and Australia have agreed to further strengthen their defense relationship, including joint development of advanced defense technologies.

Japan.title: Tobacco Companies Ordered to Pay Billions to Japanese Smokers
Japan.summary: Japan's top court ruled that tobacco company liabilities to smokers would not be limited, a move that could increase healthcare costs in the country.
